title: New Ebola Outbreak Spreads Across Borders in Central Africa
subtitle: Health officials work to stop the spread of a rare virus strain as cases reach Uganda.

summary.en: A new Ebola outbreak has caused 65 deaths in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Health officials have also confirmed a case in Uganda, raising concerns about regional spread. The outbreak is particularly worrying because it involves a rare strain of the virus. Currently, there is no licensed vaccine for this specific version of the disease. International health groups are meeting to coordinate a response to the crisis.
summary.zh: 一場新的伊波拉疫情已在剛果民主共和國造成六十五人死亡。衛生官員亦證實烏干達出現確診病例，引發對區域擴散的擔憂。此次疫情特別令人擔憂，因為涉及的是一種罕見的病毒株。目前，針對此特定版本的疾病尚無核准的疫苗。國際衛生組織正召開會議，以協調應對這場危機。

paragraphs.en: Health officials are working to control a dangerous new outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). According to The Guardian, the disease has already caused 65 deaths in the country’s Ituri province. There are currently 246 suspected cases of the illness, which is a type of haemorrhagic fever—a severe condition that causes internal bleeding and high fever. The situation has become more serious as the virus has moved across the border into Uganda. Officials in Uganda confirmed that a 59-year-old man died in a hospital in Kampala, the capital city, after traveling from the DRC.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生官員正致力於控制剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of the Congo，簡稱剛果民主共和國）境內一場危險的新型伊波拉疫情。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，該疾病已在該國伊圖里省造成六十五人死亡。目前有兩百四十六起疑似病例，這是一種出血熱，屬於會導致內出血與高燒的嚴重疾病。隨著病毒跨越國界進入烏干達，情況變得更加嚴峻。烏干達官員證實，一名五十九歲男子在從剛果民主共和國旅行後，於首都坎帕拉的一家醫院過世。

paragraphs.en: This is the 17th time the DRC has faced an Ebola outbreak since the virus was first discovered there in 1976. The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) is leading the response to the crisis. Dr. Jean Kaseya, the director general of the Africa CDC, stated that the high movement of people between the affected areas and neighboring countries makes rapid regional coordination essential. The Africa CDC has organized an urgent meeting with authorities from the DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan, as well as the World Health Organization, to discuss how to stop the virus from spreading further.
paragraphs.zh: 這是自一九七六年伊波拉病毒在剛果民主共和國首次被發現以來，該國第十七次面臨疫情爆發。非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention，簡稱非洲疾管中心）正主導這場危機的應對工作。非洲疾管中心主任卡塞亞（Jean Kaseya）博士表示，受影響地區與鄰國之間的人員高度流動，使得迅速的區域協調變得至關重要。非洲疾管中心已與剛果民主共和國、烏干達、南蘇丹的當局以及世界衛生組織召開緊急會議，討論如何阻止病毒進一步擴散。

paragraphs.en: One of the biggest challenges in this outbreak is the specific type of virus involved. Most past outbreaks in the DRC were caused by the Zaire strain of Ebola, for which vaccines and treatments already exist. However, health officials reported that laboratory tests show this outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain. According to Ars Technica, this is a significant problem because there is currently no licensed vaccine available for this specific strain. Jean-Jacques Muyembe, a leading Congolese expert who helped discover Ebola, noted that the presence of this different strain will make the medical response much more complicated.
paragraphs.zh: 此次疫情面臨的最大挑戰之一是涉及的病毒類型。剛果民主共和國過去大多數的疫情是由薩伊（Zaire）伊波拉病毒株所引起，目前已有相關疫苗與治療方法。然而，衛生官員指出，實驗室檢測顯示此次疫情是由邦迪布焦（Bundibugyo）病毒株所引起。根據《Ars Technica》報導，這是一個嚴重的問題，因為目前針對此特定病毒株尚無核准的疫苗。曾協助發現伊波拉病毒的剛果專家穆延貝（Jean-Jacques Muyembe）指出，這種不同病毒株的存在將使醫療應對工作變得更加複雜。

paragraphs.en: Experts point to several reasons why these outbreaks happen so often in this region. Dr. Michael Head, a senior research fellow at the University of Southampton, described the situation as a "perfect storm" of factors. He explained that people in the area often come into close contact with animals that carry the virus, such as bats or primates. Other factors include the movement of people between rural villages and busy urban mining towns, the tropical climate, and the large amount of rainforest in the region. These conditions make it very difficult for health workers to track and contain the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 專家指出該地區疫情頻繁爆發的幾個原因。南安普敦大學（University of Southampton）資深研究員海德（Michael Head）博士將此情況形容為多種因素交織的「完美風暴」。他解釋，該地區民眾常與攜帶病毒的動物（如蝙蝠或靈長類動物）密切接觸。其他因素還包括鄉村與繁忙礦業城鎮之間的人員流動、熱帶氣候，以及該地區廣大的雨林。這些條件使得醫護人員難以追蹤並控制疾病。

paragraphs.en: Ebola is known for its high fatality rate, meaning many people who catch it do not survive. The virus spreads through direct contact with the body fluids of infected people, such as blood or vomit. This is why the disease often spreads during funeral preparations, where family members may touch the body of a person who has died from the virus. Because of how easily it spreads, health authorities are urging people to be extremely careful and to follow safety rules during burials and when caring for the sick.
paragraphs.zh: 伊波拉病毒以高致死率聞名，這意味著許多感染者無法存活。病毒透過直接接觸感染者的體液（如血液或嘔吐物）傳播。這就是為什麼疾病常在葬禮準備期間擴散，因為家屬可能會觸摸死於該病毒者的遺體。由於病毒傳播極為容易，衛生當局呼籲民眾務必極度謹慎，並在處理喪葬事宜及照顧病患時遵守安全規範。

paragraphs.en: As the situation continues to develop, the international community is watching closely. While the DRC has experience managing past outbreaks, the combination of a rare virus strain and the movement of people across borders creates a high level of risk. The focus for the coming weeks will be on finding and treating infected individuals, educating the public on how to avoid contact with the virus, and working together across borders to prevent a larger regional crisis. For now, the priority remains the safety of the people in the affected zones and the prevention of further deaths.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢持續發展，國際社會正密切關注。雖然剛果民主共和國在處理過往疫情方面擁有經驗，但罕見病毒株與跨國人員流動的結合，帶來了高度風險。未來幾週的重點將放在尋找並治療感染者、教育大眾如何避免接觸病毒，以及進行跨國合作以防止更大的區域危機。目前，首要任務仍是確保受影響地區民眾的安全，並防止更多死亡案例發生。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where several bad events or conditions happen at the same time, making the overall result much worse.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 完美風暴（指多種不利因素同時發生，導致情況極度惡化）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個比喻用法，並非指真的天氣現象。文中：Dr. Michael Head, a senior research fellow at the University of Southampton, described the situation as a "perfect storm" of factors.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To follow or monitor the progress or movement of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（軌道），這裡作動詞，指追蹤病毒的擴散。文中：These conditions make it very difficult for health workers to track and contain the disease.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A specific version or type of a virus or bacteria.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（拉緊、過濾），這裡作生物學名詞，指病毒的不同類型。文中：However, health officials reported that laboratory tests show this outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths

quiz.question: What is the primary reason why the current Ebola outbreak in the DRC is considered more difficult to manage than previous ones?

options.A: The virus has already spread to more than five neighboring countries.
options.B: The outbreak is caused by a strain for which there is no approved vaccine.
options.C: The death toll has already surpassed all previous outbreaks since 1976.
options.D: Local health authorities in the DRC have refused to cooperate with the Africa CDC.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，過去的疫情多由 Zaire 株引起，已有疫苗與治療方法，但此次為 Bundibugyo 株，目前尚無核准的疫苗。選項 A 錯誤，文中僅提到擴散至烏干達。選項 C 錯誤，文中未將此死亡人數與歷年總數比較。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到 Africa CDC 正與 DRC 當局合作，並未提到拒絕配合。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might funeral practices in the region be a significant factor in the spread of Ebola?

options.A: Funerals often take place in crowded urban mining towns where the virus originates.
options.B: Family members are required to handle wild animals during traditional burial rites.
options.C: Physical contact with the deceased during funeral preparations facilitates viral transmission.
options.D: The tropical climate accelerates the decomposition of bodies, making them more infectious.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，伊波拉透過體液（如血液或嘔吐物）傳播，而葬禮準備過程常涉及觸摸死者遺體，這直接導致了病毒的傳播。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提到葬禮多在礦業城鎮舉行。選項 B 錯誤，文中提到的是觸摸死者身體，而非處理野生動物。選項 D 錯誤，文中雖提到氣候，但未連結至遺體分解與傳染性的關係。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does Dr. Michael Head mean when he describes the situation as a 'perfect storm'?

options.A: A weather event that is causing severe flooding in the rainforest.
options.B: A combination of various negative factors that create an especially bad situation.
options.C: A highly successful strategy that effectively controls the virus.
options.D: A sudden, unexpected change in the genetic makeup of the virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中，「perfect storm」是一種慣用語，指多種不利因素同時發生，導致災難性後果。此處指動物接觸、人口流動、熱帶氣候等因素疊加。選項 A 錯誤，這是對詞彙的字面誤解。選項 C 錯誤，這是一個負面描述。選項 D 錯誤，文中未將此詞用於描述病毒突變，而是描述環境與社會因素的集合。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the DRC?

options.A: The Ebola virus is primarily spread through the mining industry in urban areas.
options.B: The Africa CDC has successfully eliminated the Bundibugyo strain in Uganda.
options.C: The international community is no longer concerned about the Ebola situation in the DRC.
options.D: The current outbreak presents a complex challenge due to a rare strain and regional movement.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體重點在於說明此次疫情的複雜性，特別是結合了難以治療的病毒株、跨國人口流動以及地理環境因素，使得防疫工作變得困難。選項 A 僅是造成疫情的眾多因素之一。選項 B 錯誤，文中並未提到疫情已被消除。選項 C 與文章最後一段內容矛盾，文中強調國際社會仍在密切關注。
